name: Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop
name_original: Vereda de Fresneda o del Soto por el Río Guadiana — circular desde Valverde de Mérida
description: Hike the moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop, exploring diverse landscapes and the Guadiana River.
region_intro: Hiking around Valverde De Mérida offers diverse trails through a landscape characterized by natural parks, reservoirs, and riverine environments. The region features gently rolling terrain with modest elevation changes, making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Trails often traverse open countryside, providing views of the surrounding natural features. This area, part of Extremadura, provides opportunities to explore local flora and fauna within its protected zones. Best hiking trails around Valverde De Mérida The most popular hiking route is Cornalvo Reservoir Loop, an easy 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This circular path offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural parkland. Another top favourite among local hikers is Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida — circular through the Parque Natural de Cornalvo, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This route explores the Parque Natural de Cornalvo, featuring varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Valverde De Mérida is defined by reservoir paths, natural park trails, and river routes along the Guadiana. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Valverde De Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Valverde De Mérida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valverde De Mérida?
A: There are over 80 hiking trails around Valverde De Mérida. The region offers a diverse network of paths, with 38 routes considered easy, 41 moderate, and 10 more challenging, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida?
A: The hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida are predominantly easy to moderate. You'll find 38 easy routes and 41 moderate routes, making the area accessible for most hikers. There are also 10 more difficult trails for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Valverde De Mérida?
A: Yes, many trails are suitable for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.7-mile (7.5 km) circular path that offers pleasant views of the reservoir and surrounding natural parkland.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Valverde De Mérida?
A: Yes, Valverde De Mérida features several circular hiking routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291247472/presa-de-cornalvo-from-san-pedro-de-merida-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida — circular through the Parque Natural de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural park.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida?
A: The trails often lead through natural parks, past reservoirs, and along riverine environments. You can explore natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Park of the Seven Chairs&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6417421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Paintings of La Calderita&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida suitable for families?
A: Many trails in Valverde De Mérida are suitable for families, especially the easy and moderate routes. The gently rolling terrain and modest elevation changes make them accessible for various ages. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; for a family-friendly outing.

Q: What is the typical length of hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 4.7 miles (7.5 km), or longer, more challenging options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359873627/cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route, which spans over 16 miles (25 km).

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Valverde De Mérida?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the region, especially in open countryside areas. However, within protected areas like the Parque Natural de Cornalvo, specific rules may apply regarding leashes or restricted zones. Always check local signage and regulations for the specific trail you plan to hike.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Valverde De Mérida?
A: The region, particularly within the Parque Natural de Cornalvo, is known for its diverse flora and fauna. Hikers may encounter various bird species, including raptors, and potentially see mammals like deer or wild boar, especially during quieter times of the day.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Valverde De Mérida?
A: The hiking experience in Valverde De Mérida is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene natural parkland, the scenic reservoir views, and the well-maintained paths that offer a peaceful escape into nature.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. While hiking, you can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6417421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Paintings of La Calderita&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the nearby city of Mérida offers significant Roman heritage, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of Diana&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Mérida&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valverde De Mérida?
A: The best time for hiking in Valverde De Mérida is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers.
tips:
Valverde de Mérida is a charming municipality in the province of Badajoz. 
Parish Church of San Bartolomé: A beautiful 16th century Mudejar style building.
Archaeological Remains: These include dolmens and cave paintings that show human presence since prehistoric times.
Right next to the Río Guadiana there is this path called the Vereda de Fresneda or del Soto, where the river itself accompanies you for a good part of the path!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Valverde de Mérida Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is approximately 9.5 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 27 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Valverde de Mérida. You can usually find parking within the town, which serves as the primary access point for this trail.

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife habitats, to ensure their safety and respect for the local environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The region experiences diverse seasons. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the open agricultural sections, so an early start is advisable if hiking during warmer months. Winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Guadiana Natural Trail: Valverde de Mérida Loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Valverde de Mérida Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, transitioning from croplands, including irrigated fields, to holm oak dehesas. You'll primarily traverse dirt roads, especially through the upper flood plains of the Guadiana River. The broader Guadiana basin features a complex physiography with rivers and brooks.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop runs alongside the Guadiana River, which is a central feature. You'll experience a blend of agricultural landscapes and natural areas, including Mediterranean and riparian forests. The area is part of the "Río Guadiana Alto- Zújar" Site of Community Importance, highlighting its ecological value.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this loop is generally suitable for beginners and families with some hiking experience. The elevation gain is minimal (around 56 meters), and much of the route follows dirt roads, making it accessible. Always consider the distance and your group's fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The riparian scrubland along the Guadiana River is rich in plants like oleander and African Tamarix. The area is a significant biodiversity reserve, especially for birds. You might spot species such as the Golden Eagle, Bonelli's Eagle, Osprey, and various vultures, particularly during migration or wintering seasons.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop utilizes sections of the larger Camino Natural del Guadiana, covering approximately 52% of its route. This means you'll be walking on a well-established natural trail for a significant portion of your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the loop starts and ends in Valverde de Mérida, you can expect to find local amenities such as cafes or small shops within the town for refreshments before or after your hike.
